name: Mississippi Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Mississippi - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

address.description: Mississippi State Department of Health, Professional Licensure, P. O. Box 1700, Jackson, MS 39215-1700

processingTime: Varies, typically 4 to 6 Weeks after all documentation is received
renewalCycle: Every 2 years (by March 31 of odd years) + 30 CE hours

requiredDocuments.name: CDR Registration Verification
requiredDocuments.description: Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.

requiredDocuments.name: Jurisprudence Exam Score
requiredDocuments.description: Documentation of successfully passing the state-specific rules exam.

requiredDocuments.name: Official College Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from the university showing your conferred degree.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mississippi dietitian license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The initial license application fee is a non-refundable $100. The provisional license costs $50.

mainEntity.name: When does the Mississippi license need to be renewed?

acceptedAnswer.text: Mississippi regular dietitian licenses are renewed biennially (every two years) by March 31 of odd-numbered years. The renewal fee is $100.

mainEntity.name: Does Mississippi have state-specific continuing education requirements?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Regular license holders must complete 30 hours of approved continuing education every two years. Provisional license holders must complete 15 hours annually.

mainEntity.name: Does Mississippi have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Mississippi. To work in Mississippi, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Mississippi?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 3 to 4 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Mississippi while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
